text: supporter: Hello seeker: Hi! Did they send you a summary or anything of what's going on? If not, I've been struggling general depression. And overwhelming anxiety. supporter: They did not, what is causing your depression and anxiety? seeker: A cycle of anxiety.. overthinking that leads to depression. Nothing specific at the moment supporter: So just to clarify, you are having overwhelming depression and anxiety today that has been caused by nothing in particular? seeker: yes supporter: I completely understand, I have had many issues in the past with anxiety and depression. seeker: Any advice on how to push past the racing thoughts? supporter: Have you tried to talk to a professional about this, it is always helpful to talk to someone who is trained to talk about things like this with people. seeker: Yes supporter: What suggestions did they give you for coping with this? seeker: Several. Did you succeed with your issues? Or do you have any advice for a random bad day? supporter: One thing that I did was really get back into some of the hobbies that I enjoyed in order to take my mind off of things. seeker: That makes a lot of sense. Winter makes it tougher too I think.

supporter: Hello there, how are you feeling? seeker: im good. how are you? just dealing with this guy supporter: So, you are having trouble dealing with someone? seeker: yes and no. i agreed to work with him on the board when he asked me. he said he trusts me supporter: If he says he trusts you I am sure that he does. He would not have asked you to work with him if he did not. seeker: very true. not sure if he just likes me as as friend or if he wants me back. we dated then he broke up. then he asked me out again and didn't see me cuz he was busy. now he's asking me to be on the board with him. not sure if he has other motives to try to get back with me supporter: That must be incredibly confusing for you. How does this make you feel overall? seeker: confused! i'll know sooner or later what he wants. thing is i was hanging out with his friends recently who had wanted to sleep with me. he knew it and didn't like it. he liked it when i stopped talking to them supporter: How does this relate to him asking you to be on the board with him? seeker: cuz the first time he asked me out i was helping him with the board. i think it might be a way for him to spend time with me and then work his way back in. not sure if he will or not though. time will tell supporter: It is okay to feel confused and perhaps even angry about this. Have you considered talking to him and asking him straight out what his intentions are? seeker: yes but i'm afraid what i'll hear supporter: I realise that this must be difficult but isn't it best to get things out into the open? seeker: it is but hes already hurt me before. i feel i'll find out sooner or later anyways. that way i can save face supporter: I think it might be best for you to make a clean break. It sounds like you are being manipulated. seeker: possibly. i'd prob be better being away from the situation supporter: Certainly you would be. I know that it is hard but you will feel better in the long run. seeker: I think I would. I tried to break away once but they asked me to come back i feel there are benefits to it also supporter: You need to be aware that that can and will happen. Ignore it. What sort of benefits do you feel that there are? seeker: it gives me an upper edge and more clout supporter: In what way does it do that? seeker: being on the board gives me perks supporter: Do you feel that perhaps you might be using him just a little bit? seeker: yes and i think he is using me to a degree also supporter: Let me get this straight - you do have feelings for him but you are confused and you think that there's an element of mutual manipulation. seeker: yes supporter: You are definitely not the only person to feel this way! seeker: it's a difficult situation. i think it will become clearer the deeper i get into it. thing is he is the head of the board and holds the power. what it comes down to is im wondering if he has feelings for me supporter: I feel that you are paying too much attention to your position on the board. seeker: i feel it gives me an advantage i want at the moment. supporter: But that is rather manipulative of you. seeker: if you were me you would forget about him and the board supporter: Yes I definitely would do that. seeker: thing is he is encouraging me to be like that. cuz it helps him too okay. i'll try to lay low and walk away from it supporter: I think it's time for a clear severance. Do you have any other people you are interested in? Any hobbies? seeker: yes but it's less than before cuz of Covid lockdowns. so it makes me more susceptible to this- if that makes sense supporter: Ah I hear you. You feel that your options are rather more limited than previously because of the lockdown? seeker: yes supporter: That is something I believe that most of us are feeling right now so I sympathise. seeker: true. i just have to be strong about it with him. even if he likes me i don't think it's a healthy relationship i slept with one of his friends and the other one wanted to sleep with me too supporter: I can tell that you really have deep reservations and know deep down that this is not a healthy place for you to be Is he aware that this happened? seeker: yes when it comes down to it he suspected it happened his friend asked the one i slept with if it was true. he denied it. so i think he suspected it supporter: Do you feel guilty about this? seeker: now that im talking to him i do a little. it felt good at the time like i was getting back at him you can tell this is a very twisted situation

seeker: I believe that I can get over this time in my life but I need support. supporter: What are you having a problem with? seeker: My husband has cancer. I have been really depressed. supporter: I understand how you are feeling. My mom had cancer. It really helped me to join a support group. seeker: So how do you keep going without crying? How do I find a support group? I live in a rural place. supporter: Crying is natural. You have every right to have those feelings. Don't try and stop yourself from crying. seeker: Ok. How do I not cry in front of him? supporter: There are online support groups you could get involved with. seeker: Yeah but where do I find them? supporter: You can google - online cancer support groups. seeker: It makes it tough because I don't make as much money as he does/ What kind of cancer di your mother have? supporter: My mother had rectal cancer. She went through a surgery and chemo and radiation and then she went into remission. seeker: Oh he has a 25 percent of remission supporter: If you are having problems with money you might be able to qualify for food assistance through the SNAP program. Hang in there, I know it is rough but there is hope. seeker: Yeah but food I can always get. Its paying the bills. supporter: Hang in there, I know it is rough but there is hope. seeker: I know I will snap out of it and get my strength back. I always bounce back. supporter: Remember, as much as you need to be strong for him - you need to take care of yourself extra special during this time. Remember, as much as you need to be strong for him - you need to take care of yourself extra special during this time. seeker: Yeah I have been. I am thinking about rewarding myself.
seeker: Hi, how are you today? supporter: I am okay. What is going on with you? seeker: School's going badly. I really wish I had chosen a different major.
seeker: Hello, I am feeling very sad because I tested positive for Covid this week. I am in quarantine away from my family.

seeker: Hello I am very sad I am more depressed than ever supporter: Would you care to elaborate about what you're feeling sad about? seeker: I recently broke up with my girlfriend supporter: I am sorry to hear that, that sounds difficult. seeker: its been very hard on me I've spent the last few years wit her supporter: How have you been coping? That can be really hard, have you been able to talk to anyone about it? seeker: i've been trying to talk to family mainly my mom and brother supporter: Do you find that to be helpful? seeker: yes family is always there for me its only hard because I've been with my gf for so long and we've spent so much time during quarantine together supporter: That's good, it's great to have a good support system. I can imagine that's difficult, that would be a lot of close time together. Have you been able to socialize with friends or colleagues? seeker: i try to keep my personal life away from my coworkers i've talked to a few friends i'm actually going out tonight to meet some up for happy hour supporter: That's understandable about the coworkers. Going out sounds like would be beneficial, just to at least spend some time with people who care about you. seeker: yea its going to take my mind off of things

supporter: Hi there, are you having a good day? seeker: not doing good as I am stressed pretty badly due to the fact that I have been unemployed and can't find a job. supporter: I am really sorry to hear that - it is a terrible time to be out of work isn't it? Unfortunately it is happening to a lot of people seeker: Yep, the pandemic isn't helping things either. supporter: Yeah, I know an awful lot of people who have been made unemployed and there is very little work out there. Have you considered doing gig work online? You can make a bit of money there. seeker: I know as its bad all over the country. Luckily, I am able to make a little money doing surveys on MTurk. supporter: Me too! It's one of my main sources of income these days. Are you looking for other work? seeker: Yes, but online seems to be hit and miss as there are scams out there right now too. supporter: Happily I have found it to be good and honest work in general so I'd not worry too much :) seeker: Yes, I am as I have over 15 years of warehouse experience as well as forklift experience. supporter: What sort of field would you like to move into after the pandemic? seeker: I would like to getting back into the warehousing or forklift operator field. supporter: So you feel that your life is just on hiatus at the moment and you want it to get back to the way it was as soon as possible? seeker: Yes as I am getting behind on bills and its Christmas time so that makes it even tougher when I feel that I am letting my family down by not working currently. supporter: Please do not feel like that! A lot of people are in the same position and it really is not at all your fault. seeker: It's tough not to as underneath the tree, there isn't much. supporter: Have you considered contacting charities that might be able to help with Christmas presents and food? seeker: I have but they are strapped at this time and really don't have much to help with currently supporter: (I am so sorry I had to leave the room temporarily, just let me catch up :)) seeker: No worries...as I understand. supporter: Ah yes I hear you , I know that there are a lot of people out there in trouble right now. I think though that you should be able to access some food help? I have had to do that myself in the past and I do know that I felt ashamed about it. seeker: I am waiting for some help currently supporter: What sort of help are you waiting for if you do not mind my asking? seeker: Yes, it is a hit to the mindset, that's for sure. supporter: Yes, especially if you've never had to rely on that sort of help before. But there is no shame in it! seeker: I have talked to charities, gotten together with my church pastor... supporter: I am glad that you have someone outside the family that you can talk to and I hope that they are sympathetic! seeker: True, there is no shame but it is emotionally hard to deal with.

supporter: Hi seeker: Hi, I am in a bad mood and am hoping you can help :) supporter: What's wrong? seeker: My roommate has told me she wants me to move out but I have no where to go. I feel helpless. supporter: Is there a reason she wants to leave? seeker: She says I'm too critical and I complain too much. Meanwhile, she's the miserable one. supporter: Maybe you could help her feel more comfortable around you, as that is what I would do :) seeker: She's had both vaccines and watches CNN 20 hours a day...meanwhile I watch positive videos, do yoga, meditate and work all day. She's projecting. I'm trying. I'm not saying anything negative even though that's just my NY personality. She doesn't have a sense of humor though. supporter: It seems like you two don't share a lot of chemistry. But then again, you can't afford to let her leave seeker: I think she just wants to live alone here and wallow in her misery and embarrassing TV choices. lol But yes, you're right, I'm just trying to make her comfortable. And yes, we have nothing in common. I may buy a car to live in. But I'm in AZ and it's about to be 110 degrees every day... supporter: No i don't think thats a good idea, if you are still able to change her mind Maybe try to talk it out with her in a calm setting seeker: She screamed at me one day about 2 weeks ago, but hasn't mentioned it since. But, I do know she still wants me to go...the problem is if I bring it up, she gets really angry. It's like I can't win. I think she thinks when I try to talk about it, it means I want to leave and since there's nothing either of us can do about it, she hates me even more...because it implies I can't stand her. If that makes any sense. She's extremely non-confrontational. (except for that one day where she just let it all out--hates my personality apparently) supporter: Yeah, if she is non-confrontational that means that you will have to initiate it Because she won't and she will end up leaving seeker: But I don't want to make her mad again. Sometimes she just forgets things. lol Maybe she'll forget that she wants me to leave. The funny thing is, she just bought a trailer....and I offered to live in it. She didn't go for that idea. supporter: yeah but you can't really take such a gamble tbh. And why did she buy it, is it because she wanted you to live in it? seeker: She bought it as a horse trailer. So she can both live in it when she goes on the rode and carry her horses too. But she hasn't even used it since she bought it months ago. Do you know how long we're supposed to chat for btw? road*, not rode. lol sorry Ah, 15 minutes...I see it says on the left. supporter: We were supposed to chat for at least 10 messages each i think seeker: Oh good, I think we've done that? I tried this study before but don't know if I got paid...don't think so. supporter: It takes a while i think like a week, but i am not sure seeker: Okay, cool. Well, thanks for the advice. This is my reality right now actually. supporter: Yeah, if she wanted to leave she would've done so long ago in my opinion bye seeker: bye :)

seeker: Hi there, could you please give me some advice? supporter: Hello, would you like to explain your situation and we can go through it together seeker: I feel that I am blowing my future because I am just not scoring well on tests these days. I just don't feel motivated to work as hard as I know that I should. supporter: So you don't feel motivated and you are not scoring well, have I understood that correctly? seeker: Yes. I know that the two things are probably related but I just do not know what to do about them. supporter: What are you currently studying? seeker: I am studying microbiology. I do not know that I have the passion for it that I once did though. supporter: Your motivation and how you are studying is probably related, you are right. What attracted you to microbiology in the first place? seeker: I have always found it fascinating and have an uncle who is a microbiologist. But these days I feel I made the wrong decision. Is it too late to change? supporter: It is never too late to change anything. However, i hear that you have always found it fascinating,have you considered talking to your uncle and seeing if you can find what you loved about it again? seeker: Yes that does sound like good advice. I do not want to turn by back on my studies of the past years. But my grades are really poor and I am not sure what to do about that.
seeker: Good afternoon. Hope you are surviving he virus supporter: Hello, I hope you are as well. Is anything the matter? seeker: I am just looking for a friend to chat with. Just feeling lonely.

seeker: Hi supporter: hello how are you ? seeker: I'm in depression because my exam is near and I can't finsh my studies. supporter: can i ask you ,why you can't finish seeker: It is because i'm very behind in my work. supporter: try to catch up with your studies give more time then you feel better seeker: How am I going to catch up on my work? supporter: i think you need to be confident about your studies then you fell better seeker: How can I be confident? supporter: i think you need to take more online classes those can help you seeker: Ok I will try to get in an online class if I can. supporter: because same think happen with me and i took online classes those help me a lot *thing seeker: Ok how much time do you spend on online classes? supporter: i spend like 3 hours a day on online class seeker: Thanks for the idea do you have any more tips? supporter: i think you need to take help from your family or friend those who know all about your studies seeker: Thanks for helping me I might pass the exam after all.

supporter: Hello seeker: hi supporter: how may i help you today? seeker: my girlfriend broke up with me supporter: why? seeker: she taught i was cheating on her supporter: Was you? seeker: no i wasn't supporter: what made her think you was? seeker: she saw our chat history which honestly looks incriminating supporter: why do you feel that way? seeker: because we kind of flirt with each other supporter: ok i am a little confused. you saw who chat history with who? seeker: my girlfriend saw my chat with another woman supporter: ok i understand now. What was you and this other woman talking about? I understand how you are feeling breakups are hard going through seeker: favorite sexual position yes, i am feeling very sad at the moment supporter: That does sound incriminating I am sorry you going through this Have you tried talking to her and apologized for your actions seeker: i tried to but she is not giving me the chance supporter: Maybe you should give her some space for a while and let her get her thoughts together and then try to talk to her seeker: but how long will that space be supporter: maybe a couple days seeker: its been up to a week already supporter: maybe you try to talk to her again today seeker: i am planning on it supporter: if that does not work then maybe it will be best to just focus on yourself i know how hard that can be but sometimes its for the best seeker: that will be hard because i am soo used to her being around supporter: I can understand that but the only way you can work it out with her if both of you two just talk seeker: yes you are right

supporter: Hello, how are you feeling today? seeker: HI supporter: Is there anything that you'd like to talk about? I am happy to listen if you have a problem. seeker: Yeah its about my personal problem can you give me any suggestions supporter: Well, I'd need to know what your personal problem was first. Can you tell me? seeker: Im in depression over past 4 months supporter: I am very sorry to hear that. I have suffered from depression too so I empathise. Can you tell me what is making you feel depressed? seeker: I lost my job because of the covid 19 supporter: Is the global pandemic having an effect on you? I know it has been making a lot of people feel bad seeker: it makes me feel so depressed supporter: I am very sorry to hear that. Do you have any other form of financial support? seeker: yes it is, the worst period in my life supporter: I am really very sorry. I have been feeling down lately too so I do understand. Do you have any hobbies that can help you right now? seeker: and i did not get any other job till now supporter: Do you have family that you can talk these things out with? seeker: yes i have a family but they are not ready to talk to me about this problem at all supporter: Are they feeling bad too? I think that everyone is feeling quite down right now. Am I right in thinking that your family is too? seeker: yes they are supporter: It is so difficult right now, isn't it? I just feel defeated sometimes. Have you thought of getting any outside help? There is free counselling available. seeker: yes i can't afford our livelihood supporter: There should be help out there. There are foodbanks, if you need those. seeker: no im not asking any help supporter: why not? seeker: because in my place there are same situation for many people supporter: I understand that. I hope I was able to be of some help, at least. seeker: so they are also suffering supporter: Maybe next year will be a better year seeker: which makes me feel so depressed supporter: Thank you for talking and I hope you feel okay seeker: yeah i think so supporter: you need to press quit now :) I can't do it this end seeker: would you like to donate some money to me supporter: no I would not. But press quit and you will be paid. seeker: oh that's ok, that's why i'm not asking help from the other people

seeker: Hello,is anone there? supporter: Yes sorry, the app was misbehaving. How are you doing today? seeker: I don't know. I'm shaking because I don't know what I'm going to do. supporter: Im sorry to hear that, but going to do about what situation specifically? seeker: I know you're busy prepping for the holiday but I described my problem earlier. It should be listed either at the top or on the left side of your screen. I know you're probably prepping for the holiday. I didn't mean to interrupt your Sunday. supporter: Im sorry but we do not get those promps We just get to talk pretty much, we have to find out whats going on. You are not interrupting anything. seeker: Here! Last Christmas I bought the condo of my dreams. This April 2020 I lost my job due Covid 19. My unemployment is ending and my savings are depleted. I've recived an eviction notice and don't know what to do. Help! Have you ever been evicted? supporter: Where are you that you are able to be evicted? You should not be able to be evicted from your home during this time in pretty much every state. Have you tried contacting anyone for help other than your landlord? seeker: I'm still in my condo but I no longer have a job, umemployment has run out and my savings are all gone.

supporter: hello how are you today? seeker: Hi there. I'm OK but have had a difficult time at work recently supporter: i am sorry to hear that, would you like to talk about it? seeker: Yes I have been given what I can only describe as ridiculous feedback by my manager and it had annoyed me greatly. I'm thinking about complaining about it. supporter: It is always hard to hear any bad feedback, do you feel that you could speak with your manager about it and try to resolve the feelings? seeker: I could do but this seems to be something that she has an issue with and I wouldn't want it to damage our working relationship. She basically said she doesn't like the way I sit in meetings and smile when I'm thinking about something? supporter: Could you possibly go to her and tell her that you have heard the feed back and you are sorry to hear that it bothers her that you did not realize it and it is something you will work on becoming more aware of. You seem like such a nice person i am sure if you approached it the right way you would not cause any damage. seeker: I'm thinking perhaps I should go to a different manager and voice these concerns. I don't see how I sit in meetings or how I smile when I'm thinking to be constructive work based feedback it's really frustrating it's clear this person just doesn't like me supporter: Going to another manager may be a great idea! Maybe the manager could address the issue with her and help her to realize there is nothing wrong with a having happy employee like yourself. seeker: yeah that's true I mean I'm almost thinking this manager needs to be retrained because this video isn't work related is really quite petty an actually makes me consider not working for her in future. The only problem is I need my job I need a steady income but this person just seems to have it out for me. supporter: Yes i can see where that would be a problem. seeker: perhaps I'm just making too much of a big deal about this. how would you react if someone gave you similar feedback in work? supporter: Do you think you could speak to this other manager about possibly transferring positions so you are not working under your current manager? seeker: I haven't actually thought about that as a solution but that might work actually. I think maybe I'm overthinking this but I've taken it very personally. supporter: Well you have a right to feel how you do, everyone takes things differently. It would bother me but i would proably try to get to know her better to see why she has such a problem with others being happy, maybe she has some problems going on in her life and has no one to share these problems with if she is not happy it may come as a struggle to see others happy all the time. seeker: I think that's an interesting perspective. she might have issues herself and be taking them out on members of her team in work which is quite sad really. Do you think I should just be blunt and ask her the question? supporter: I would let her know nicely that some things she said has been hurtful and ask if there is anything on her mind that she would like to chat about that you would be glad to talk with her. seeker: so be supportive but also make it clear that what she has said has had an impact on me? supporter: Yes you are such a positive person that i think you being supportive will help open up communication, and yes you should make it known that she has said something that has affected you personally and in addressing it may help her not treat others that way in the future. seeker: ok great I'll definitely try that and try and put this advice into practice supporter: In the mean time try to not take it personally you really seem like a nice person so i dont think she meant to cause you any harm. seeker: yeah maybe I should stop being too serious, I'm trying to see the funny side of it. Thanks again!

seeker: Hello supporter: Hey, how are you today? Anything on your mind? seeker: I'm OK. Just dealing with a lot of unknowns. supporter: Yeah? Can you tell me a bit about these known unknowns? seeker: I work for cruise industry and it's been a bit tough since whole COVID situation started. supporter: Ah man, thats pretty unlucky isn't it, I'm in the same situation, but I think yours is a bit worse. seeker: Yes, I'm just always worried that I can loose my job. supporter: Hey, I feel you there on the anxiety, you'll definitely get through this. seeker: It's just constantly to many unknowns, and it feels like it's not even getting better. supporter: Thats what anxiety is all about, the best you can do is figure out how to address those unknowns. seeker: But how do I address them, how would I deal with this? supporter: I lost my job, started coding, doing odd-jobs, and learning survival skills. That is how I prepared for my unknowns. seeker: That sound great, just need to look for a back up plan. supporter: Doesn't hurt to be prepared for anything, even the worst case scenarios! Don't sweat what other people think about you, its your life. seeker: I guess it's a good time to start looking into creating a plan.

seeker: Too Much Work Pressure supporter: I understand... What kind of work do you do? seeker: Administration Work supporter: I understand that. How else are You seeker: I am female and feminism problems also there during that time i am getting very anger supporter: I am Jason by the way... How am I speaking with? seeker: U r good What type of work u r doing What about me ? supporter: Well I am male although I understand having issues everyday. Are things slow at your work? seeker: Sure as a working people everyday issue will be there ... i am right My work is Slow ? supporter: How are you feeling right now? seeker: Not so bad , but Ok supporter: Well what's going on with your work? What's causing so much pressure in your life? seeker: Too much administrative work like preparing reports for various departments and send mail to clients There is no proper communication from top level people so happening error That time i am getting angry supporter: I can understand that, kind of makes you feel mistrustful? seeker: Yes, you are correct , So i home i like to listen some good music to reduce my tension DO like music? What type of music do u like supporter: I love music seeker: Great Me Too , really awesome supporter: I honestly listen to mostly rap... lol what about you? What do you like? Although I pretty much listen to anything. Second in line is Country lol seeker: i like instrumental music , like mild guitar and some other supporter: What is your favorite instrument? seeker: Piano and Guitar supporter: I love piano, my favorite anyway... Do you play? seeker: Some times i like to here some Indian instrument also No, i will not but i like to listen Sorry i will not play , but i like to listen Do u know to play? supporter: I understand that. I tried to play once, but it was difficult. Variety is the spice of life. seeker: Yes you are correct me too tried one time but i can't supporter: I got further with piano than guitar, but that still wasn't far. I tick with just listening now. seeker: That is great pleasure to be supporter: I do enjoy it. I listen to music while I do everything. I run every morning as well so music is great. The running helps to relieve stress. What other hobbies do you have? seeker: I am other hobbies is take care of my kid... She is an angle to me.. i love her lot and she is great to me supporter: I understand that. They definitely bring joy into our lives. Do you just have one? seeker: Yes , only one .. what about you ? and your family supporter: Well I am married with a wife and daughter... Been together for 15 years almost. seeker: Awesome , that is happy to hear , So you enjoying the life with beautiful family supporter: I am actually at the office today. I couldn't the day off. Do you celebrate Christmas? seeker: Yes, little , some work is there doing remotely supporter: Merry Christmas! seeker: Merry Christmas!!! supporter: I just needed to tell you that in case you haven't heard it from anyone today. seeker: Yes, you little special person from others Shall we finish the conversation

supporter: Hello there! How are you today? seeker: Im okay How do you want to do this? supporter: Sure! Can you please tell me how can i help you today? seeker: Ive been feeling like other people dont like me supporter: If I understand you correctly, do you assume people dislike you even if you can't tell for sure? seeker: Yes, i automatically assume I'm valueless in the other persons eyes, no matter what they say or do to the contrary supporter: It seems you are a bit low on self-confidence. I have been there too. If I may ask you, does this feeling persist all the time irrespective of the situation or it happens after any particular event - say a test or some outdoor game for example? seeker: Yeah, I've been texting my friend and he doesn't reply to me for hours and it makes me feel like he isnt as enthusiastic about our friendship as i am which hurts supporter: Right, the feeling of being ignored can be quite overwhelming. Have you tried speaking with your friend over phone or video call, assuming you have access to the technology? seeker: We have decent phone conversations, I guess I'm just frustrated and sad about my worthiness, now that im typing it out it sounds not nearly as bad Thank you supporter: Great! You are worthy! Remember that always :) Is there anything else I can help you with? seeker: Haha thank you What do you think i should do when i feel this way next time supporter: Good question! Don't let the feeling consume you. After all , you are dealing with another person who genuinely might be busy or held up with some important work. seeker: Thats true supporter: So, even if they don't respond, just think they are driving or working, and try to avoid thinking that you are being ignored. seeker: I always assume since im not busy that they arent too I guessits a little self involved

supporter: How can I be of support? seeker: I've lost my job due to COVID and I'm really worried. supporter: That is so difficult because you lost your job. supporter: Hello, how are you doing today? seeker: I am feeling angry today about work. supporter: Oh no, what happened to make you feel this way? seeker: My coworker expects me to do things the way she does and is constantly critical. I just want her to understand that I am able to do things my way and it is still okay. I told her this and she ignored me. supporter: I'm sure that's terribly frustrating having someone treat you like that. Is this something that your boss is aware of? seeker: I have not told her yet. I do not want to get her into trouble and I want to have a good relationship with her. supporter: Can you discuss it with her in a respectful way to get her to understand that you're not trying to undermine her but that this is how you work most efficiently? And maybe mention that you don't want to involve the boss? seeker: That's something I could try. I really care about my coworker and it makes me so angry that she treats me this way. supporter: I think sometimes it's easy to jump to anger when you feel hurt. It might be worth telling her how you feel and also maybe ask why she thinks you should be doing things her way. Maybe there's a give and take in this situation. Some of her methods could be beneficial. seeker: Do you have any advice on how to start my conversation with her? I have found some of her ideas to be beneficial. I just would like my ideas expressed as well. I feel we can learn from each other. supporter: Tell her the things that you like about her method and maybe suggest some of your methods to her as well. Maybe she'll find them to be more efficient. Make it a partnership rather than a fight for whose method is better. She could feel threatened by you so it's worth digging into those feelings with her and proving to her that you're working toward the same goal. seeker: That's a great idea. It is possible. I am the new kid on the block. I will try by being non confrontational. Any specific ideas to how I can do that? She gets offended easily. I want her to know I am helping. supporter: I'd suggest how much she's helped you since you are in fact the new kid on the block. And tell her that you want to work with her to have the most efficient partnership possible and use each of your talents to the best of your ability. Then you can lay out how you work best and see where it goes from there. seeker: Thanks! I will try that. You made me feel so much better! supporter: I'm so glad, these can be frustrating situations but sometimes it's worth looking at the other person's perspective. seeker: I agree. Sometimes you just have to take a step back and have a conversation with someone not in the thick of it. supporter: Absolutely, I have no emotional stake in this. The two of you do and that can cloud things sometimes. seeker: I think people get territorial when they have worked somewhere and a new person comes in. I see that now. I am optimistic we will work it out and be a great team!

In sum, I'm the only one left in his corner, but I feel like he's slipping away and becoming completely dysfunctional. It's tough with privacy laws for me to even talk to his doctor to see what's going on. I did send his doctor a long email, however, so hopefully he'll make whatever adjustments need to be made. I can understand how you feeling and that you are trying to help your friend. you are a very good friend for doing that. seeker: I have talked to him repeatedly. Literally, within a day or two, I hear from a mutual friend or family member that he's reverted back to doing things that are sociopathic. supporter: Have you asked him about him taking his medication? seeker: I have. He tells me that he's taking it regularly (or most of the time). I challenged him on it and told him it can't be "most of the time." That's why I sent his doctor a long email, explaining things to him, to see if he can't intervene and try to straighten him out. supporter: ok. dealing with someone with bi-polar disorder can be really tough and challenging but it is not there fault that they are acting the way they are. They just honestly can not help it. I know you are doing everything you can to help him and to stop him from acting that way. seeker: You're right. Mental illness, in all of its forms, is especially challenging. Physical illness is more easy to understand, of course. supporter: you are right. So do not think he is doing it on purpose. Do not let it bring you down or hurt your feelings in anyway. When did this exactly start happening? seeker: Thank you. I think you understand the sadness and anxiety in feeling powerless in this situation (or mostly powerless). I have no real expertise in this area, but by contacting his doctor, maybe he can reevaluate whether the medicine and dosage needs to be changed. Funny you ask. He's a grown man - 35-years old. I've never known him to have this issue, which seems so strange to me. supporter: I understand a lot. seeker: It started about 14 months ago - at least that's when people started alerting me to strange behavior. I thought people who were bi-polar contracted the illness earlier in their life.
